data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260507T180521-74d6594e/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260507T180521-74d6594e/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260507T180521-74d6594e/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260507T180521-74d6594e/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260507T180521-74d6594e/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.

## Decomposition depth cap
This bead is already at decomposition depth 2. Do not create another child layer; if it is still too large, reject the split with a short explanation and write no_changes_rationale.txt instead.

  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-224ffcd2">
    <title>try: count no-progress only for valid implementation attempts</title>
    <description>
PROBLEM
The drain loop still treats no-progress mostly as base_rev == result_rev after a failed implementation attempt. That is too broad now that ddx work has pre-claim intake, pre-dispatch lint, routing preflight, review malfunctions, operator parks, and other non-implementation outcomes. Those states should not consume no-progress retry budget or trigger long cooldowns.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1964-1978 implements shouldSuppressNoProgress with only three checks: disrupted, transient outcome reason, and base/result equality.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1980-1987 only treats transport, quota, routing, timeout, merge_conflict, lock contention, and no viable provider as transient; it omits intake blocks, decomposition, claim races, routing preflight, auth/tool setup, review errors, and needs_human/operator states.
- cli/internal/agent/try/attempt.go:230-249 increments no_changes metadata before the work layer has a reusable valid-implementation-attempt classifier.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1199-1215 and cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1262-1275 apply no-progress cooldowns from separate branches instead of a shared eligibility decision.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a reusable no-progress eligibility helper, either in cli/internal/agent/try or cli/internal/agent/work, that answers whether the implementer had a real opportunity to inspect/modify/verify code.
- Count no-progress only for valid implementation attempts with checkout/context established and an implementer result available.
- Exclude intake rewrites/blocks/decomposition, claim races, routing preflight, quota, transport, auth/tool setup, review errors, needs_human/operator-required states, and other system failures.
- Route eligible no-progress stops through the StopCondition NoProgress vocabulary from ddx-89ab3fda.
- Preserve existing behavior for genuine no-commit implementation attempts.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change review verdict taxonomy beyond marking review/system outcomes no-progress-ineligible.
- Do not schedule repair cycles.
- Do not alter max-cost handling.

DEPS
Depends on ddx-89ab3fda for the NoProgress StopCondition vocabulary.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. A reusable helper classifies valid implementation attempts for no-progress accounting, and production cooldown/no_changes paths call it.
2. TestStopCondition_NoProgress_IgnoresIntakeRoutingReviewAndOperatorStates covers intake block, decomposition, claim race, routing preflight, quota, transport, auth/tool setup, review-error, and needs_human/operator-required cases.
3. TestStopCondition_NoProgress_CountsRealImplementationNoCommit covers a valid implementation attempt where base_rev == result_rev and no system/operator classifier applies.
4. Existing TestSuppressNoProgress_HonorsTransientReasons, TestNoChangesWithoutRationaleDoesNotUseNoProgressCooldown, and TestLoop_GenuineNoProgress_NoDefaultCooldown still pass.
5. No-progress decisions surface StopCondition NoProgress in the work-layer test path without changing existing durable execute-loop-retry-after metadata keys.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run "TestStopCondition_NoProgress|TestSuppressNoProgress|TestNoChangesWithoutRationaleDoesNotUseNoProgressCooldown|TestLoop_GenuineNoProgress" -count=1 passes.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... passes.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, story:10, area:agent, area:try, area:work, kind:fix, reliability, adr:024, spec:FEAT-010, split-from:ddx-9228a484</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-5cb6e6cd" spec-id="FEAT-010" base-rev="e1e0f83acf7b25b27adc82a21091407c0cb8e2a5" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260507T180521-74d6594e"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-010" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-010-task-execution.md">Feature: Task Execution</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>
